name: jobs
context: Run batch workloads and scheduled tasks in Kubernetes with Jobs and CronJobs
info: Jobs run one-off or batch workloads to completion. Unlike [[kubernetes/deployments]] which keep Pods running indefinitely, Jobs create Pods that execute a task and terminate. CronJobs run Jobs on a recurring schedule. Agents use Jobs for database migrations, data processing, backups, report generation, and any task that runs to completion. CronJobs automate recurring tasks like nightly backups, cache warming, and periodic cleanup. This skill covers Job manifests, parallelism, completions, backoff limits, CronJob schedules, concurrency policies, and failure handling.
triggers: run a one-off task in kubernetes
run a database migration in kubernetes
set up a cron schedule in kubernetes
body:
# Jobs and CronJobs

Jobs run Pods that execute a task and terminate. CronJobs run Jobs on a schedule.

## Job

```yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: db-migrate
  namespace: production
spec:
  completions: 1            # number of successful completions needed
  parallelism: 1            # number of Pods running concurrently
  backoffLimit: 3            # retry attempts before marking as failed
  activeDeadlineSeconds: 600 # timeout — kill after 10 minutes
  ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 3600  # auto-delete Job 1 hour after completion
  template:
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never   # REQUIRED: Never or OnFailure
      containers:
        - name: migrate
          image: myregistry/migrator:v1.2.0
          command: ["./migrate", "up"]
          env:
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: db-credentials
                  key: connection-string
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 200m
              memory: 256Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 1000m
              memory: 1Gi
```

## Parallel Jobs

For batch processing with multiple workers:

```yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: process-data
spec:
  completions: 10     # need 10 successful completions
  parallelism: 3      # run 3 Pods at a time
  backoffLimit: 5
  template:
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
        - name: processor
          image: myregistry/data-processor:latest
          command: ["./process", "--batch-index=$(JOB_COMPLETION_INDEX)"]
          env:
            - name: JOB_COMPLETION_INDEX
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.annotations['batch.kubernetes.io/job-completion-index']
```

With `completionMode: Indexed`, each Pod gets a unique index (0 to completions-1) for work partitioning.

## CronJob

```yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: nightly-backup
  namespace: production
spec:
  schedule: "0 2 * * *"           # 2:00 AM daily
  timeZone: "America/New_York"     # k8s 1.27+
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid        # don't run if previous is still running
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3    # keep last 3 successful Jobs
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 3        # keep last 3 failed Jobs
  startingDeadlineSeconds: 300     # skip if missed by more than 5 minutes
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 2
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 3600
      template:
        spec:
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
          containers:
            - name: backup
              image: myregistry/backup-tool:latest
              command: ["./backup.sh"]
              env:
                - name: S3_BUCKET
                  value: my-backups
                - name: AWS_REGION
                  value: us-east-1
              resources:
                requests:
                  cpu: 500m
                  memory: 512Mi
```

## Cron Schedule Syntax

```
┌───────── minute (0-59)
│ ┌─────── hour (0-23)
│ │ ┌───── day of month (1-31)
│ │ │ ┌─── month (1-12)
│ │ │ │ ┌─ day of week (0-6, Sun=0)
│ │ │ │ │
* * * * *
```

| Schedule | Meaning |
|----------|----------|
| `*/5 * * * *` | Every 5 minutes |
| `0 * * * *` | Every hour |
| `0 2 * * *` | Daily at 2:00 AM |
| `0 0 * * 0` | Weekly on Sunday midnight |
| `0 0 1 * *` | Monthly on the 1st at midnight |
| `0 9 * * 1-5` | Weekdays at 9:00 AM |

## Concurrency Policies

| Policy | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| `Allow` | Multiple Jobs can run concurrently (default) |
| `Forbid` | Skip new Job if previous is still running |
| `Replace` | Cancel running Job and start new one |

## kubectl Commands

```bash
# Create a Job
kubectl apply -f job.yaml

# Create a quick one-off Job
kubectl create job test-job --image=busybox -- echo "Hello from Job"

# Create a Job from a CronJob template (trigger manually)
kubectl create job manual-backup --from=cronjob/nightly-backup

# List Jobs
kubectl get jobs -n production
kubectl get cronjobs -n production

# Watch Job progress
kubectl get jobs -w

# View Job logs
kubectl logs job/db-migrate

# Describe (shows events, completion status)
kubectl describe job db-migrate
kubectl describe cronjob nightly-backup

# Delete completed Jobs
kubectl delete job db-migrate
kubectl delete jobs --field-selector status.successful=1
```

## Restart Policies

| Policy | Behavior | Use When |
|--------|----------|----------|
| `Never` | Create a new Pod on failure | Logs from each attempt are preserved |
| `OnFailure` | Restart container in same Pod | Save resources, but lose logs from previous attempts |

**Do not use** `Always` (the default for Deployments) — Jobs require `Never` or `OnFailure`.

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `KUBECONFIG` | Path to kubeconfig file |
| `KUBE_NAMESPACE` | Default namespace |

sections.title: Jobs
sections.body: Jobs create Pods that run to completion. Set `completions` for how many successful Pod runs are needed. Set `parallelism` for concurrent Pods. Set `backoffLimit` for max retries. Set `activeDeadlineSeconds` for a timeout. Set `ttlSecondsAfterFinished` for automatic cleanup. restartPolicy must be Never or OnFailure. Use Indexed completionMode for work partitioning.

sections.title: CronJobs
sections.body: CronJobs create Jobs on a cron schedule. Schedule uses standard cron syntax (minute, hour, day, month, weekday). Set `concurrencyPolicy` to control overlap: Allow (default), Forbid (skip if running), Replace (cancel and restart). Set `startingDeadlineSeconds` to skip missed runs. Set `successfulJobsHistoryLimit` and `failedJobsHistoryLimit` to control how many completed Jobs are kept. Manually trigger with `kubectl create job <name> --from=cronjob/<cronjob>`.

sections.title: Failure handling
sections.body: When a Job Pod fails: with restartPolicy=Never, a new Pod is created (up to backoffLimit). With restartPolicy=OnFailure, the container restarts in the same Pod. After backoffLimit failures, the Job is marked Failed. activeDeadlineSeconds provides a hard timeout. For CronJobs, failed runs are tracked via failedJobsHistoryLimit. Debug failed Jobs with `kubectl describe job <name>` and `kubectl logs job/<name>`.
